model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch to AdamW with a higher learning rate to improve early-step optimization efficiency under the fixed 256-step budget while keeping the change minimal and low-risk.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Revert to the better-performing optimizer/LR baseline and add a meaningful cosine schedule over the actual fixed-step budget so learning-rate decay is active within 256 steps instead of being effectively flat.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Disable BatchNorm in the flat MLP to reduce optimization friction in the short 256-step regime, while also removing the already-negative scheduler change to isolate whether normalization removal improves early convergence.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore BatchNorm (which was strongly beneficial) and remove weight decay to reduce underfitting pressure in the short fixed-step regime, targeting better validation loss from faster fit.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With weight decay removed and only 256 steps available, a modest LR increase may improve early optimization speed without the instability seen at much larger LR.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the better LR and reduce Adam beta2 so second-moment adaptation responds faster within only 256 steps, which can improve short-horizon optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step evaluation, doubling batch size increases the number of training examples seen within the same 256 updates, which can improve validation loss; restoring the default Adam beta2 keeps optimization stable for the larger-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size further should expose the model to more unique training examples within 256 updates, potentially improving generalization at this short optimization horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue the successful fixed-step strategy by increasing batch size again so each of the 256 updates covers more data, which may further lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The recent gains suggest the run is data-exposure limited under fixed steps; increasing batch size again should further increase effective sample coverage across the 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has consistently improved fixed-step performance; moving to 2048 should maximize data throughput per update and may continue lowering validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Scaling batch size has remained consistently positive, so testing 4096 is a direct continuation to maximize per-step data coverage under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At very large batch size, the current LR is likely conservative; increasing LR should improve optimization progress per step in the fixed 256-update budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Now that optimization is much stronger (large batch and higher LR), modestly increasing MLP width should improve representational capacity and may further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After successful width scaling, adding one more MLP block should increase nonlinear capacity and may further reduce validation loss with the now-strong training regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After substantial capacity increases, adding a very small weight decay can improve generalization without materially slowing optimization in the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With very large-batch training and deeper/wider network, a further LR increase may improve convergence speed within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Revert to the strongest observed setting after recent regressions: LR 1e-3 and zero weight decay matched the current high-batch, higher-capacity regime best.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given the strong monotonic gains from larger batches under fixed-step evaluation, testing 8192 is the highest-leverage remaining micro change for additional data coverage per update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 8192, a higher learning rate is likely better matched to the reduced gradient noise and may yield additional loss reduction within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
